political.s: IHG does not make party political donations and avoids partisan political involvement. The company maintains neutral political engagement through its employee and franchisee PACs rather than direct corporate contributions.

charity.s: IHG operates True Hospitality for Good, a flagship charitable program focused on disaster relief, hospitality skills building, food security, and environmental sustainability. The company's Giving for Good month (September) mobilizes thousands of employees annually to volunteer and support charitable causes, with IHG contributing approximately $400,000 per participation cycle.
charity.focus: Disaster relief, hospitality skills development, food security, environmental sustainability

environment.s: IHG has committed to environmental sustainability through its Journey to Tomorrow 2030 plan, promoting water and energy reduction, waste minimization, and biodiversity conservation. However, as a large hotel franchisor with mostly franchised properties, actual implementation varies widely across locations.

labor.s: IHG shows mixed labor practices with some NLRB cases on record and hospitality industry challenges including wage stagnation and high turnover. The company reports commitment to safe working conditions and Equal Employment Opportunity but faces typical hospitality sector ergonomic and safety concerns.

dei.s: IHG demonstrates strong pro-DEI commitment, achieving a 100% score on the Human Rights Campaign Corporate Equality Index for seven consecutive years and supporting multiple employee resource groups (BERG, SOMOS, PATH, Out & Open). The company partners with HBCUs and implements mandatory unconscious bias training.

animals.s: Not applicable - hotel operations do not involve animal testing or production.

guns.s: IHG does not have a system-wide firearm prohibition policy. Individual franchised properties set their own policies in compliance with local laws. At IHG Army Hotels, firearms are specifically prohibited per military regulations and stored with Military Police.

privacy.s: IHG maintains GDPR compliance and data protection standards with privacy officers and procedures for EU data subjects. However, the company collects substantial guest and employee data for loyalty programs and operations, and retains information for extended periods for regulatory and litigation purposes.

execPay.s: CEO Elie Maalouf earned approximately $13.91 million in 2025 compensation (89.5% bonuses). Hotel industry CEO-to-average worker pay ratio widened to approximately 205:1 in 2024, reflecting significant executive compensation relative to line-level hospitality workers earning under $58,000 annually.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
